Long.description: 64-bit signed integer, the default numeric type for integers in Clojure

Double.description: 64-bit IEEE 754 floating-point, the default type for decimal literals

String.description: Immutable sequence of Unicode characters, same as Java strings

Boolean.description: Boolean value type; nil and false are falsy, everything else is truthy

Keyword.description: Symbolic identifier prefixed with colon, often used as map keys and enum values

Symbol.description: Identifier used to name things, resolved to vars in namespaces

PersistentVector.description: Immutable indexed collection with near-constant-time access and update

PersistentHashMap.description: Immutable hash map with structural sharing for efficient updates

PersistentList.description: Immutable singly-linked list, the fundamental sequence type

PersistentHashSet.description: Immutable set of unique values with hash-based lookup

LazySeq.description: Lazily evaluated sequence that computes elements on demand

Atom.description: Thread-safe mutable reference type for uncoordinated synchronous state

Ratio.description: Exact rational number represented as numerator/denominator pair

BigInt.description: Arbitrary-precision integer for numbers exceeding Long range
